properties.text:
3x3 Grid  —  Batch Processing Skeleton

This workflow produces 9 tiles in a single IMAGE batch [9, H, W, C].
As-is it runs crop+stitch (identity) to verify the math produces a clean output.

TO USE WITH A BATCH-CAPABLE AI MODEL:
  Break the wire from TileCrop.tiles → TileStitch.tiles.
  Insert your model between them. Examples:
    • SDXL KSampler with Tile ControlNet  (processes full batch)
    • Any img2img node that accepts a batch input
    • SeedV2 / custom API node

For PER-TILE models (NB2, ChatGPT-Image-2): use the nb2_2x2_per_tile workflow
as a reference and scale up — add 9 TileExtract nodes (index 0..8) + TileCollect.
Index map:   0 1 2
             3 4 5
             6 7 8

As-is it runs crop+stitch (identity) to verify the math produces a clean output.

For PER-TILE models (NB2, ChatGPT-Image-2): use the nb2_2x2_per_tile workflow
as a reference and scale up — add 9 TileExtract nodes (index 0..8) + TileCollect.
Index map:   0 1 2
             3 4 5
             6 7 8

properties.text:
↓  INSERT BATCH AI MODEL HERE  ↓
Break the TileCrop.tiles → TileStitch.tiles wire.
Your model receives a batch of 9 tiles and must return
a batch of 9 processed tiles (same or larger size).

properties.text:
GRID SIZE GUIDE

2x2  →  4 tiles   (standard upscale)
3x3  →  9 tiles   (this workflow)
4x4  →  16 tiles  (max detail, high VRAM)

Change grid in TileCrop to switch.
TileStitch auto-adapts to any grid.
